introSection: Fury 325 is a steel giga roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. The ride opened on March 28, 2015. It was built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Carowinds officially announced the addition of Fury 325 on August 21, 2014. That same month, track pieces arrived at the park.

Vertical construction officially began in September 2014 with the addition of the first track piece. In November 2014, the lift hill began rising. The highest point of the ride was topped off on December 4, 2014. In January 2015, construction was completed when the last track piece was installed.

On February 23, 2015, a lighting package was installed in the station and on the lift hill.

Fury 325 performed its first test runs on March 4, 2015.

Seats on the first three trains were auctioned to raise money for the Cam Newtown Foundation. The media day was held on March 25, 2015 (or "3/25" in shorthand). Fury 325 then opened on March 28.

sections.title: Ride closure
sections.content:
On June 30, 2023, a crack was discovered on one of the supporting columns, near where it met the track. In videos shared online, the upper section had become completely severed from the rest of the column and moved as the train passed over it. The coaster was closed after staff were made aware of the crack. The cracked support was subsequently wrapped in plastic.

Fury 325 reopened on August 11 after repairs were complete and the ride had been inspected.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Fury 325 passes over and under two walkways from the parking lot to the park's new main entrance. During the construction of Fury 325, the park entrance was rebuilt. It was built at a cost of $30 million.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The front car from one of the trains was on show at B&M's booth during the 2015 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

sections.title: Significance
sections.content: Fury 325 is the tallest roller coaster with a traditional lift hill, standing 325 feet tall (thus the namesake). It is tied with Steel Dragon 2000 as the fastest roller coaster without a launch. It is the longest steel roller coaster in North America. It is the tallest, fastest and longest roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.content: Like Millennium Force at Cedar Point, the amusement park sim game Planet Coaster has a B&M-type giga coaster called Rage inspired by Fury 325.

fullText:
Fury 325 is a steel giga roller coaster located at Carowinds in Charlotte, North Carolina, USA. The ride opened on March 28, 2015. It was built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

Carowinds officially announced the addition of Fury 325 on August 21, 2014. That same month, track pieces arrived at the park.

Vertical construction officially began in September 2014 with the addition of the first track piece. In November 2014, the lift hill began rising. The highest point of the ride was topped off on December 4, 2014. In January 2015, construction was completed when the last track piece was installed.

On February 23, 2015, a lighting package was installed in the station and on the lift hill.

Fury 325 performed its first test runs on March 4, 2015.

Seats on the first three trains were auctioned to raise money for the Cam Newtown Foundation. The media day was held on March 25, 2015 (or "3/25" in shorthand). Fury 325 then opened on March 28.

On June 30, 2023, a crack was discovered on one of the supporting columns, near where it met the track. In videos shared online, the upper section had become completely severed from the rest of the column and moved as the train passed over it. The coaster was closed after staff were made aware of the crack. The cracked support was subsequently wrapped in plastic.

Fury 325 reopened on August 11 after repairs were complete and the ride had been inspected.

Fury 325 passes over and under two walkways from the parking lot to the park's new main entrance. During the construction of Fury 325, the park entrance was rebuilt. It was built at a cost of $30 million.



The front car from one of the trains was on show at B&M's booth during the 2015 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

Fury 325 is the tallest roller coaster with a traditional lift hill, standing 325 feet tall (thus the namesake). It is tied with Steel Dragon 2000 as the fastest roller coaster without a launch. It is the longest steel roller coaster in North America. It is the tallest, fastest and longest roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard.

Like Millennium Force at Cedar Point, the amusement park sim game Planet Coaster has a B&M-type giga coaster called Rage inspired by Fury 325.
